name: ICMA Green Bond Principles
description: The Green Bond Principles (GBP) are voluntary process guidelines that recommend transparency and disclosure and promote integrity in the development of the Green Bond market by clarifying the approach for issuance of a Green Bond.

useOfProceeds.description: The utilisation of the bond proceeds for Green Projects should be appropriately described in the legal documentation for the security. All designated Green Projects should provide clear environmental benefits, which will be assessed and, where feasible, quantified by the issuer.

eligibleCategories.name: Renewable energy
eligibleCategories.description: Including production, transmission, appliances and products. Covers solar, wind, geothermal, biomass, hydropower, marine energy, and other renewable sources.

eligibleCategories.name: Energy efficiency
eligibleCategories.description: Such as in new and refurbished buildings, energy storage, district heating, smart grids, appliances and products.

eligibleCategories.name: Pollution prevention and control
eligibleCategories.description: Including reduction of air emissions, greenhouse gas control, soil remediation, waste prevention, waste reduction, waste recycling and energy/emission-efficient waste to energy.

eligibleCategories.name: Environmentally sustainable management of living natural resources and land use
eligibleCategories.description: Including environmentally sustainable agriculture, animal husbandry, climate smart farm inputs, fishery and aquaculture, forestry, preservation or restoration of natural landscapes.

eligibleCategories.name: Terrestrial and aquatic biodiversity conservation
eligibleCategories.description: Including the protection of coastal, marine and watershed environments.

eligibleCategories.name: Clean transportation
eligibleCategories.description: Such as electric, hybrid, public, rail, non-motorised, multi-modal transportation, infrastructure for clean energy vehicles and reduction of harmful emissions.

eligibleCategories.name: Sustainable water and wastewater management
eligibleCategories.description: Including sustainable infrastructure for clean and/or drinking water, wastewater treatment, sustainable urban drainage systems and river training and other forms of flooding mitigation.

eligibleCategories.name: Climate change adaptation
eligibleCategories.description: Including efforts to make infrastructure more resilient, e.g., development of climate resilient information systems such as climate observation and early warning systems.

eligibleCategories.name: Circular economy adapted products, production technologies and processes
eligibleCategories.description: Such as the design and introduction of reusable, recyclable and refurbished materials, components and products; circular tools and services; and/or certified eco-efficient products.

eligibleCategories.name: Green buildings
eligibleCategories.description: Meeting regional, national or internationally recognised standards or certifications for environmental performance.

processForProjectEvaluation.description: The issuer of a Green Bond should clearly communicate to investors the environmental sustainability objectives, the process by which the issuer determines how the projects fit within the eligible Green Project categories, and the related eligibility criteria.
requirements: Clearly communicate environmental sustainability objectives of the green projects
Describe the process for determining project eligibility
Define and disclose eligibility criteria, including exclusion criteria if applicable
Disclose any green standards or certifications referenced in project selection
Describe process for identifying and managing environmental and social risks associated with projects
recommendedDisclosures: Complementary information on processes to identify and manage perceived social and environmental risks
Information on alignment of projects with official or market-based taxonomies

managementOfProceeds.description: The net proceeds of the Green Bond, or an amount equal to these net proceeds, should be credited to a sub-account, moved to a sub-portfolio or otherwise tracked by the issuer in an appropriate manner, and attested to by the issuer in a formal internal process.
requirements: Track net proceeds in a sub-account, sub-portfolio, or equivalent
Attest to tracking in a formal internal process linked to lending/investment operations
Disclose intended types of temporary placement for unallocated proceeds
Balance of unallocated proceeds should be periodically adjusted
Recommend use of an external auditor or other third party to verify tracking
managementOfProceeds.temporaryPlacements: Pending full allocation, unallocated proceeds should be held in cash, cash equivalents, or other liquid instruments, consistent with issuer's investment policy.

reporting.description: Issuers should make readily available and keep current information on the use of proceeds, to be renewed annually until full allocation, and on a timely basis in case of material developments.

allocationReporting.description: Should include list of projects, amounts allocated, expected impact, and any unallocated balance.
requirements: List of projects to which Green Bond proceeds have been allocated
Amounts allocated per project or category
Share of proceeds used for financing vs. refinancing
allocationReporting.frequency: Annual until full allocation, and in case of material developments

impactReporting.description: Issuers should use qualitative and, where feasible, quantitative performance indicators to report on the environmental impact of projects.
requirements: Use quantitative performance indicators where feasible
Report on a project-by-project basis or on a portfolio basis

metrics: Reduction in number of private vehicles

externalReview.description: The GBP recommend that issuers appoint (an) external review provider(s) to confirm the alignment of their Green Bond or Green Bond programme with the four core components of the GBP.

types.name: Second Party Opinion (SPO)
types.description: An institution with environmental expertise that is independent from the issuer assesses the issuer's Green Bond Framework, evaluating alignment with the GBP and/or other green criteria.

types.name: Verification
types.description: An issuer can have its Green Bond, associated Green Bond framework, or underlying assets independently verified by qualified parties. Verification focuses on alignment with internal or external criteria.

types.name: Certification
types.description: An issuer can have its Green Bond or associated Green Bond framework or use of proceeds certified against a recognised external green standard or label.

types.name: Green Bond Rating/Scoring
types.description: An issuer can have its Green Bond or associated Green Bond framework rated or scored by qualified third parties, such as specialised research providers or rating agencies.

relatedGuidelines.name: Green Bond Principles — Harmonised Framework for Impact Reporting
Guidelines for Green, Social, Sustainability and Sustainability-Linked Bonds External Reviews
EU Green Bond Standard (EuGBS) — Regulation (EU) 2023/2631
relatedGuidelines.notes: Voluntary EU standard based on GBP principles, requiring EU Taxonomy alignment for at least 85% of proceeds
